title: Quiz: Classical Reservoir Computing
description: Test your understanding of reservoir computing fundamentals

questions.question: What is the key advantage of reservoir computing over traditional RNNs?

options.text: Only the readout layer is trained, making training very fast
Reservoirs can only process time series data

questions.explanation: In reservoir computing, the recurrent reservoir weights are fixed and randomly initialized. Only the linear readout layer is trained, typically using simple ridge regression, avoiding costly backpropagation through time.

questions.question: What is the Echo State Property (ESP)?

options.text: The reservoir produces the same output for all inputs
The reservoir state depends only on input history, not initial conditions
The reservoir echoes back the input signal
The reservoir amplifies all signals equally

questions.explanation: The Echo State Property ensures that the reservoir 'forgets' its initial state over time and its current state depends only on the history of inputs, not on how it was initialized.

questions.question: What condition on the spectral radius ρ(W) guarantees the Echo State Property?
questions.explanation: A spectral radius less than 1 (ρ < 1) is a sufficient condition for ESP. This ensures that the reservoir dynamics are contractive and the influence of initial conditions decays over time.

questions.question: What does 'fading memory' mean in reservoir computing?

options.text: The reservoir forgets all inputs immediately
Recent inputs have more influence on current state than distant past inputs
The memory of the system decreases over training

questions.explanation: Fading memory means the influence of past inputs decays over time—recent inputs affect the current state more than inputs from the distant past. This is essential for processing temporal sequences.

questions.question: In reservoir computing, the recurrent reservoir weights are trained using backpropagation.
questions.explanation: False. The reservoir weights are fixed and randomly initialized. Only the output/readout weights are trained, typically using simple linear regression or ridge regression.

questions.question: What mathematical method is typically used to train the readout layer in ESNs?

options.text: Ridge regression (linear regression with L2 regularization)

questions.explanation: Ridge regression (W_out = (X^TX + λI)^{-1}X^TY) is the standard method for training ESN readouts. It has a closed-form solution, making training extremely fast.

questions.question: What are the three main components of a reservoir computer?

options.text: Input layer, reservoir (fixed), and readout layer (trained)
Convolutional, pooling, and fully connected layers

questions.explanation: A reservoir computer has three components: the input layer (maps inputs to reservoir), the reservoir (fixed random recurrent network), and the readout layer (trained linear output).

questions.question: Which are advantages of reservoir computing? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Reservoir computing offers (a) fast training via linear regression, (b) no gradient issues since reservoir is fixed, and (d) natural fit for temporal data. It does NOT always outperform deep learning—each has its strengths.
